steps.title: Use perpendicular slopes
steps.content: A radius to a tangent point is perpendicular to the tangent line. Since line $u$ has slope $\frac{5}{4}$, the radius from $(2,5)$ to $(a,-4)$ has slope $-\frac{4}{5}$.

steps.title: Write the slope equation
steps.content:
Use the slope formula for the radius.
$$\frac{-4-5}{a-2}=-\frac{4}{5}$$
So
$$\frac{-9}{a-2}=-\frac{4}{5}$$
